explanationZhTw: 這裡指「烘焙」咖啡豆，不是一般把食物放進烤箱那種泛用的烤。
generatedExample: The roasting of the beans filled the room with a rich aroma.

explanationZhTw: 表示「不由自主地、下意識地」，動作不是出於刻意，而是自然反應。
generatedExample: She smiled involuntarily when she heard the good news.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，表示「切斷、阻斷」，也就是讓氣味像突然被隔開一樣消失。
generatedExample: The loud noise cut off our conversation.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `rash` 不是「皮疹」，而是「魯莽、草率」；`rash act` 指「魯莽的行為」。
generatedExample: Skipping the safety check was a rash act.

explanationZhTw: `attendance` 在這裡指「出席次數／到場紀錄」。
generatedExample: My attendances at the club are recorded every week.

explanationZhTw: `spare time` 指「空閒時間」。
generatedExample: I read in my spare time.

explanationZhTw: `taste for solitude` 指「喜歡獨處」；這裡是在說任何偏好單獨行動的跡象都可疑。
generatedExample: She has a taste for solitude after a long day.

explanationZhTw: 這是虛構的 Newspeak 詞，意思是「個人主義、特立獨行」。
generatedExample: The regime treated any sign of OWNLIFE as suspicious.

explanationZhTw: `balminess` 指「和煦、溫暖宜人的感覺」。
generatedExample: The balminess of the afternoon made everyone stay outside.

explanationZhTw: `on impulse` 指「一時衝動地、憑直覺地」。
generatedExample: On impulse, he booked the flight that night.

explanationZhTw: `wandered off` 指「漫無目的地走開／走遠」。
generatedExample: We wandered off after lunch and found a small cafe.

explanationZhTw: `labyrinth` 這裡是比喻用法，指像迷宮一樣複雜的街道；整個片語意為「迷宮般的倫敦」。
generatedExample: She got lost in the labyrinth of back streets.

explanationZhTw: `hardly bothering` 在這裡表示「幾乎不在意／幾乎沒去想」。
generatedExample: He answered the question, hardly bothering to look up.

explanationZhTw: 在此特指「無產階級／底層群眾」，不是一般的複數名詞用法；在《1984》裡有政治語境。
generatedExample: The proles had little power, but they were still the majority of the city.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 come back to him 是片語動詞，意思不是「回來找他」，而是「反覆浮現在他腦中／一直想起來」。
generatedExample: That warning kept coming back to him all day.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 vague 不是單純「模糊」的抽象概念，而是用來形容地帶輪廓不清、難以明確辨認。
generatedExample: From the hill, the town looked vague in the morning fog.

explanationZhTw: swarmed 表示「成群湧來、擠滿」，帶有大量人群快速流動的畫面。
generatedExample: Fans swarmed around the entrance after the concert.

explanationZhTw: in full bloom 在這裡是慣用說法，意思是「正值青春、風華正盛」，不是字面上的「盛開」。
generatedExample: She was in full bloom during her college years.

explanationZhTw: boarded up 是片語動詞，意思是用木板封住、釘板遮住，常用於破窗或荒廢建築。
generatedExample: After the storm, several shops were boarded up.

explanationZhTw: guarded 在這裡表示「有所保留、戒備」，整個片語是「帶著小心試探的好奇」。
generatedExample: He answered with guarded curiosity, not wanting to reveal too much.

explanationZhTw: "that's all very well" 是常見的轉折語，意思接近「聽起來不錯／很合理，但……」，通常用來委婉地反駁前一句話。
generatedExample: That's all very well, but we still need a clear plan.

explanationZhTw: "in my place" 不是字面上的「在我的地方」，而是「站在我的立場、處境中」的意思。
generatedExample: If you were in my place, you would understand the pressure.

explanationZhTw: "criticize" 是「批評、指責」；這裡指對別人的做法提出負面評語。
generatedExample: It is easy to criticize, but harder to solve the problem.

explanationZhTw: "ain't got" 是非正式口語，等於 "don't have" 或 "doesn't have"，這裡意思是「沒有」。
generatedExample: I ain't got enough time to finish this today.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `jest` 是 `just` 的方言/古老拼法，意思是「正是、沒錯、就是這樣」。
generatedExample: That's just it: we need more time to finish properly.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `jest` 是 `just` 的方言/古老拼法，意思是「正好、就在那裡」。
generatedExample: The bus stop is just where it should be, next to the station.

explanationZhTw: 形容聲音刺耳、尖銳、很強烈；這裡是用來形容說話聲帶有壓迫感。
generatedExample: Her strident tone made everyone in the room uncomfortable.

explanationZhTw: 表示「帶有敵意的、不友善的」；這裡不是正式攻擊，而是氣氛上的排斥感。
generatedExample: He got a hostile response when he asked the same question again.

explanationZhTw: 表示警戒、提防；這裡指一種本能的、不完全明說的戒心。
generatedExample: There was a note of wariness in her voice when she mentioned the deal.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指身體或姿態「突然繃緊、僵住」；不是一般的「變硬」，而是瞬間的防備反應。
generatedExample: He noticed a stiffening in her shoulders when the subject came up.

explanationZhTw: 表示「不明智的、不聰明的」；語氣比單純的錯誤更強，帶有風險判斷。
generatedExample: It would be unwise to ignore the warning.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，這裡是「偶然遇到、碰上」的意思，不是字面上「跑進去」。
generatedExample: I ran into my old teacher at the station yesterday.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是一般的「紙張」，而是指身分證明文件、證件。
generatedExample: The officer asked to see my papers before letting me through.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「等等、諸如此類」；用來省略未列完的相似內容。
generatedExample: He kept asking about my job, my family, and so on and so forth.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 route 指「路線、路徑」；unusual route 就是「不尋常的回家路線」，暗示可能被懷疑。
generatedExample: He took an unusual route to avoid the crowded streets.

explanationZhTw: 片語，表示「使人注意到、引起注意」；這裡帶有負面後果，因為會招來監視。
generatedExample: The bright sign will draw attention to the entrance.

explanationZhTw: 「騷動、混亂」；這裡指整條街一下子陷入混亂狀態。
generatedExample: The station was in commotion after the announcement.

explanationZhTw: 「警告的喊叫聲」；指四面八方傳來大聲提醒危險的聲音。
generatedExample: We heard yells of warning before the cars reached the corner.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 shooting 不是「射擊」，而是「迅速衝進去」的意思，整體是在形容人們慌忙躲進門口。
generatedExample: When the alarm sounded, everyone was shooting into the nearest shelter.

explanationZhTw: 「迅速抱起／抓起」；表示動作很快地把小孩抱走。
generatedExample: She grabbed up her bag and ran for the train.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 whipped ... round 表示「快速地把……包住／圍住」；不是字面上揮動鞭子。
generatedExample: He whipped the towel round his shoulders to keep warm.

explanationZhTw: 「像手風琴那樣可折疊的／皺褶狀的」；這是形容外觀的比喻用法，較不常見。
generatedExample: The machine had a concertina-like cover that folded neatly shut.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是警告別人「小心、注意」；不是字面上的「看出去」。
generatedExample: Look out, there's a car coming.

explanationZhTw: 英式口語，指「先生、老闆、頭兒」；這裡帶有街頭味和對話感。
generatedExample: All right, guv'nor, I'll do it now.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `bang` 不是「爆炸」的名詞，而是口語副詞「正好、直接地」；整體意思是「正好在頭頂上方」。
generatedExample: The helicopter flew bang overhead.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是命令語氣，意思是「立刻躺下、趴下」。
generatedExample: Lay down and keep your head low.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「Steamer」不是字面上的「蒸汽船」或「蒸汽機車」，而是對火箭炸彈的綽號，屬於依上下文才看得出的特殊用法。
generatedExample: People on the street nicknamed the noisy missile a 'steamer'.

explanationZhTw: 「proles」是《1984》裡的專用詞，指無產階級或底層平民，不是一般日常英文最先學到的用法。
generatedExample: The proles worked long hours and had little political power.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「迅速撲倒在地」，是為了躲避危險；重點是動作很快，不是字面上的「把自己扔到臉上」。
generatedExample: When the gunfire started, he flung himself on the floor.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「instinct」指「直覺、天生的警覺」，強調人在沒有明顯線索下也能提前感知危險。
generatedExample: She had an instinct that something was wrong before anyone spoke.

explanationZhTw: 「supposedly」表示「據說、號稱」，帶有說法未必完全可信的語氣。
generatedExample: The bridge was supposedly closed, but cars were still crossing it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「heave」不是一般的「嘆氣」或「用力抬起」，而是指地面因爆炸衝擊而隆起、震動。
generatedExample: The blast made the ground heave beneath our feet.

explanationZhTw: 「fragments」是「碎片」，這裡專指破裂後的小玻璃片。
generatedExample: We found fragments of glass on the kitchen floor.

explanationZhTw: `walk on` 是片語動詞，意思是「繼續往前走」；這裡不是單純指「走路」而已，而是帶有「不停下來、接著前進」的語氣。
generatedExample: She walked on after answering the phone.

explanationZhTw: `demolish` 是「徹底摧毀、拆毀」；常用於建築物或結構被破壞到幾乎不能再用的情況。
generatedExample: The old warehouse was demolished last year.

explanationZhTw: `plume` 在這裡不是「羽飾」，而是指一股長條狀、像羽毛般升起的煙或霧，屬於情境中的特殊用法。
generatedExample: A plume of steam rose from the kettle.

explanationZhTw: `streak` 這裡指「一道痕跡、一條細長的色帶」；不是抽象的「一陣子」，而是具體可見的線狀痕跡。
generatedExample: There was a streak of paint across the table.

explanationZhTw: `severed` 表示「切斷、截斷」；在這裡特別指手腕被切開，語氣比一般的 `cut` 更正式也更強烈。
generatedExample: The cable was severed during the storm.

explanationZhTw: `stump` 在這裡是指「殘端、截斷後留下的部分」；不是樹樁，而是身體被截斷後剩下的那一截。
generatedExample: The doctor examined the stump carefully after the surgery.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「調低」，而是「轉進、拐進」一條街或巷子。
generatedExample: We turned down a quiet lane and found a small bakery.

explanationZhTw: 形容骯髒、卑劣或令人不舒服的；這裡強調街頭景象的陰暗與低俗。
generatedExample: The hotel looked sordid after years of neglect.

explanationZhTw: 指「經常去、常出入」某個地方；不是字面上的「頻繁」。
generatedExample: The cafe was frequented by local artists and students.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是慣用說法，表示「擠滿了、塞滿了」，不是字面上的窒息。
generatedExample: The station was choked with commuters at rush hour.

explanationZhTw: 在此表示「看清楚、辨認出」；常用來說視線不夠清楚但仍能分辨。
generatedExample: I could barely make out the road sign in the fog.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是物理上的「吸收」，而是指全神貫注、深深投入的狀態。
generatedExample: She listened with absorption to every word of the story.

explanationZhTw: 指激烈爭吵或口角，語氣比一般的 disagreement 更強、更正式。
generatedExample: A brief altercation broke out over the last seat on the train.

explanationZhTw: 表示「正要、幾乎要」發生某事；這裡是幾乎要動手打起來。
generatedExample: We were on the point of leaving when the storm started.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `bleeding well` 不是字面上的「流血」，而是英式粗口式加強語氣，意思接近「給我好好地／非常地」；整句是在很不耐煩地催促對方認真聽。
generatedExample: Bleeding well pay attention when I'm talking.

explanationZhTw: `listen to` 是常見片語動詞，表示「聽從、專心聽」；這裡不是單純聽見，而是要求對方把話聽進去。
generatedExample: Please listen to the instructions before you start.

explanationZhTw: `ain't` 是非常口語、非標準的否定形式，這裡等於 `hasn't/haven't won`；整體意思是「還沒中獎／還沒贏過」。
generatedExample: That ticket ain't won anything yet.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `'as` 是口語／方言式寫法，省略了開頭的 `h`，等於標準英語的 `has`。整句是在回應對方、表示「是啊，的確是這樣」的語氣。
generatedExample: He said the rumor was true, and I replied, “Yes, it’s been confirmed, then!”

explanationZhTw: 這裡的`'as`是口語／方言寫法，等於 `has`。整句是在說「不是這樣」，語氣很口語，也帶有模糊的否定強調。
generatedExample: 'I 'as not ready,' he said, meaning 'I has not / I am not ready.'

explanationZhTw: `'ome` 是方言拼寫，等於 `home`。這裡不是特殊詞義，而是用來表現說話者的口音與出身背景。
generatedExample: Back 'ome we eat dinner very early, which means 'back home'.

explanationZhTw: `'ole lot of 'em` 是口語／方言，等於 `the whole lot of them`，意思是「全部、整批」。這裡強調他把所有東西都一起記下來。
generatedExample: She brought the 'ole lot of 'em to the table, meaning 'the whole lot of them'.

explanationZhTw: `reg'lar` 是 `regular` 的方言／省音寫法，意思是「固定地、規律地、定期地」。在這裡表示他按固定頻率記錄。
generatedExample: I visit my grandmother reg'lar, meaning 'regularly'.

explanationZhTw: `as the clock` 是慣用說法，表示「非常準時、很有規律」。這裡不是字面上的「像時鐘」，而是在強調動作的固定與可靠。
generatedExample: He arrives as the clock every Monday, meaning he is always right on time.

explanationZhTw: 口語用法，意思是「幾乎、差不多」。這裡不是字面上的「很接近」，而是表示接近正確答案。
generatedExample: I was pretty near right about the answer.

explanationZhTw: 英式口語中的強調語，語氣很粗，類似「該死的、見鬼的」。這裡只是用來加強情緒，不是指真的流血。
generatedExample: He left the bleeding door open again.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「最後是……／尾號是……」。常用來說明號碼、字詞或結果的結尾部分。
generatedExample: My phone number ended in 84.

explanationZhTw: 「got it all down」在這裡是口語用法，意思是「把它全部記下來／寫下來」。
generatedExample: She got the address down before the train left.

explanationZhTw: 「in black and white」表示「白紙黑字、寫得很清楚」，強調內容有明確的書面證明，不是口頭說說而已。
generatedExample: It was in black and white, so he could not deny the agreement.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `pack it in` 不是字面上的「把東西收進去」，而是口語用法，表示「住手、別鬧了、停下來」或「放棄／收工」。在這句話裡，是第三人要對方停止現在的行為。
generatedExample: After arguing for ten minutes, she told him to pack it in and go home.

explanationZhTw: 指「抽獎／彩券」這類定期舉行的博彩活動；在這裡是小說中的公眾活動名稱。
generatedExample: People in my neighborhood buy a ticket for the weekly lottery.

explanationZhTw: 在《1984》裡指「無產階級／平民大眾」；這是小說中的專用詞，帶有政治階級含義，不是一般日常用語。
generatedExample: The proles were kept busy with cheap entertainment.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純的「無害」；而是指能安撫、麻痺痛苦或起安慰作用的東西，屬於較文學化的用法。
generatedExample: For him, music was an anodyne after a hard day.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「靠這個維持生計／以此為業」。
generatedExample: She made a living by repairing bicycles.

explanationZhTw: 指「實際發放（款項）」；這裡是把獎金真的付給得主的意思。
generatedExample: The company paid out the bonus at the end of the year.

explanationZhTw: 「proles」在這裡是《一九八四》裡對無產階級、底層勞工大眾的稱呼，屬於帶政治色彩的特定用法。
generatedExample: The party fears the proles because they outnumber everyone else.

explanationZhTw: 「cling on to」表示緊緊抓住、勉強維持；這裡是指勉強守住一點希望。
generatedExample: She clung on to the hope that her brother would return.

explanationZhTw: 英式英文中「pavement」指人行道，不是鋪路材料；此處是指街邊的人行道。
generatedExample: We stood on the pavement and waited for the bus.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，指在證據不足時仍選擇相信；這裡是說相信路上的人還有人性是一種信念。
generatedExample: Buying the old house without a survey was an act of faith.

explanationZhTw: 指主要幹道、交通要道；語氣較正式，表示附近有一條重要的大馬路。
generatedExample: The hotel is located on the city's main thoroughfare.

explanationZhTw: 指吵雜刺耳的喧鬧聲，常暗示聲音混亂、很吵。
generatedExample: We could hear a din from the street outside.

explanationZhTw: 指舊貨店、雜貨店，賣二手或雜物的商店；這裡是指賣舊物的店。
generatedExample: I found the lamp in a junk-shop near the station.

explanationZhTw: 昏暗、骯髒又有點破舊的小酒館；`dingy` 強調環境陰暗不潔。
generatedExample: We stopped at a dingy little pub after the concert.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指真的結霜，而是指看起來像被霜蓋住一樣，實際上只是灰塵讓窗戶變得模糊。
generatedExample: The bathroom mirror was frosted over after the hot shower.

explanationZhTw: `bristled` 在這裡是「像鬃毛般豎起」；整個片語在形容鬍子硬挺地往前翹。
generatedExample: The dog's fur bristled forward when it sensed danger.

explanationZhTw: 可前後擺動的門，常見於店家入口。
generatedExample: He pushed through the swing door and stepped inside.

explanationZhTw: 突然想到；某個念頭在腦中浮現。
generatedExample: It occurred to me that I had left my phone on the train.

explanationZhTw: 最後僅存的連結／聯繫，帶有比喻意味，指還能把人和過去世界接起來的人或事。
generatedExample: These letters are the last links to her childhood home.

explanationZhTw: 被徹底消滅、清除；常用來表示大量殺害或完全除去。
generatedExample: The virus wiped out most of the crop.

explanationZhTw: 政治清洗；指用強硬手段把不受歡迎的人從組織、政府或社會中排除。
generatedExample: The regime carried out purges against its critics.

explanationZhTw: 思想上的完全屈服；不再保有自己的判斷或觀點。
generatedExample: He refused any intellectual surrender to propaganda.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》中的專有詞，指無產階級／一般平民；在這裡是帶有小說世界觀色彩的用法。
generatedExample: In the novel, a prole is one of the ordinary working-class people.

explanationZhTw: 瘋狂般的一時衝動；形容不理性、近乎失控的念頭。
generatedExample: A lunatic impulse made him quit his job that same day.

explanationZhTw: 勉強搭上話、硬著頭皮去認識對方；屬於比較少見的慣用語。
generatedExample: He tried to scrape acquaintance with the guests at the table.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `lest` 是較正式、偏文學的「以免、唯恐」，表示他急著離開是為了避免自己開始害怕。
generatedExample: She spoke softly lest the baby wake up.

explanationZhTw: `proles` 是《一九八四》裡對平民階層的專門稱呼，屬於作品內的特殊詞彙，不是一般英語裡的日常名詞。
generatedExample: In the story, the proles live in a different part of the city.

explanationZhTw: `pass unnoticed` 表示「沒有被注意到、悄悄發生而不引人注意」，這裡指他的舉動太反常，不可能不被發現。
generatedExample: He hoped the mistake would pass unnoticed.

explanationZhTw: `attack of faintness` 指「突然一陣頭暈、虛弱」，是比較委婉、較老派的說法。
generatedExample: She pretended to have an attack of faintness to leave the room.

explanationZhTw: `din` 指「吵雜聲、喧鬧聲」，不是單純的聲音，而是讓人覺得煩躁的巨大噪音。
generatedExample: The din from the factory was hard to ignore.

explanationZhTw: `eyeing` 這裡不是字面上的「有眼睛」，而是「盯著看、打量」，帶有懷疑或審視的語氣。
generatedExample: The guard was eyeing the crowd suspiciously.

explanationZhTw: `altercation` 指「爭吵、口角」，比一般的 `argument` 更正式，也常帶一點激烈或對立的感覺。
generatedExample: He had an altercation with the cashier over the bill.

explanationZhTw: `knot` 這裡不是「結」，而是指「一小群、一撮人」聚在一起。
generatedExample: A knot of students waited outside the classroom.

explanationZhTw: 方言或口語拼法，等於標準英語的 asked，也就是「問、詢問」。
generatedExample: I arst him where the meeting was held.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 civil 不是「公民的」，而是「有禮貌的、客氣的」；civil enough 就是「夠客氣、夠有禮貌」。
generatedExample: Please be civil enough to answer the question directly.

explanationZhTw: 副詞，表示「好鬥地、挑釁地、帶攻擊性地」。
generatedExample: He pugnaciously challenged every point in the discussion.

explanationZhTw: 英式口語，指「酒吧、酒館」；在某些語境下也可指愛喝酒的人，但這裡是前者。
generatedExample: We stopped at the boozer for a quick drink after work.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，帶有強烈不耐煩或驚訝語氣，意思接近「到底是什麼鬼／究竟是什麼」。
generatedExample: In hell's name are you doing here at this hour?

explanationZhTw: 英式常用容量單位，約等於一品脫；在這裡也可指「一杯啤酒」。
generatedExample: We stopped at the pub for a pint after work.

explanationZhTw: 指酒吧侍者或酒保，負責在酒吧服務飲品的人。
generatedExample: The barman poured me a glass of water and asked if I wanted anything else.

explanationZhTw: 口語感嘆語，意思大致是「聽他在那裡說！」或「瞧他那副德行！」，帶有嘲諷語氣。
generatedExample: Ark at him boasting as if he invented the job.

explanationZhTw: 酒保；在酒吧裡調酒、賣酒的人。
generatedExample: The barman remembered my order from yesterday.

explanationZhTw: 品脫，英制容量單位；這裡指一品脫的飲料。
generatedExample: I ordered a pint of beer after work.

explanationZhTw: 夸脫，英制容量單位，等於四分之一加侖。
generatedExample: The recipe calls for a quart of milk.

explanationZhTw: 夸脫，英制容量單位，等於四分之一加侖。
generatedExample: The recipe calls for a quart of milk.

explanationZhTw: 加侖，英制容量單位；這裡用來說明更大的容量。
generatedExample: We bought a gallon of water for the trip.

explanationZhTw: ABC；這裡不是字母本身，而是指最基本、入門的知識。
generatedExample: If you want to code well, start with the A, B, C of the language.

explanationZhTw: 酒吧侍者；酒保。
generatedExample: The barman wiped the counter and asked what I wanted.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指「很快」，而是指說話簡短、帶點不耐煩地。
generatedExample: She answered shortly and turned back to her work.

explanationZhTw: 公升；容量單位。
generatedExample: Please buy a litre of milk on your way home.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是指「供應、販售」（飲料）。
generatedExample: This cafe serves tea and sandwiches all day.

explanationZhTw: 架子；層板。
generatedExample: Put the plates back on the shelf after washing them.

explanationZhTw: 英式容量單位，常用來指一杯啤酒，約 568 毫升。
generatedExample: He ordered a pint at the pub.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是指「一直重複說、堅稱」，不是一般單純的「持續」。
generatedExample: He persisted in saying the price was too high.

explanationZhTw: 方言／口語說法，意思是「給我倒、斟一杯」。標準說法會更像 `draw off` 或直接說 `serve me`。
generatedExample: The bartender drew off a pint for me.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是英式口語的加強語氣詞，帶有不耐煩或抱怨的味道，相當於「該死的／見鬼的」。
generatedExample: It's bleeding cold in here.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「住在樹梢」，而是帶有比喻意味的說法，指非常久遠、原始或落後的生活狀態。
generatedExample: People joke that their grandparents were living in the treetops compared with modern technology.

explanationZhTw: `glance at` 表示「瞥一眼、匆匆看一下」，動作很快，通常只是短暫看過去。
generatedExample: She gave the menu a quick glance at before ordering.

explanationZhTw: `customers` 是指「顧客、客人」，這裡是酒吧裡的客人。
generatedExample: The shop was busy, with several customers waiting at the counter.

explanationZhTw: 指一陣大笑、哄笑；這裡不是單純「喊」，而是形容很多人同時發出的笑聲。
generatedExample: A shout of laughter broke out when the speaker made a joke.

explanationZhTw: 指不安、局促或心理上的不自在。這裡是因為 Winston 進來而產生的緊張感。
generatedExample: She felt uneasiness before the interview started.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「進入、到場」的意思，不是常見的「條目、入口」以外的用法。
generatedExample: His entry into the room changed the mood immediately.

explanationZhTw: 形容下巴或臉上有短鬍渣，帶白色鬍碴的樣子；這是偏描述性的複合形容詞。
generatedExample: He had a white-stubbled chin after skipping a shave.

explanationZhTw: 指臉因害羞、尷尬、激動或天氣等原因變紅。這裡表示臉色泛紅。
generatedExample: Her cheeks flushed pink when everyone looked at her.

explanationZhTw: 指把身體或臉轉開；這裡表示他把注意力移開、轉身離開視線。
generatedExample: He turned away so no one could see his reaction.

explanationZhTw: 指小聲咕噥、自言自語，通常別人不容易聽清楚。
generatedExample: She walked down the hall, muttering to herself.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「不小心撞到」的意思，不是「偶然遇見」那種較常見的用法。
generatedExample: I bumped into my neighbor at the store.

explanationZhTw: 指用手抓住對方的手臂；這裡的 gently 表示動作輕柔，帶有安撫意味。
generatedExample: She caught him gently by the arm before he fell.

explanationZhTw: 「offer」在這裡是「主動提出、請你接受」的意思，屬於比較正式的用法；整句是在禮貌地問對方要不要喝的東西。
generatedExample: She offered me a seat near the window.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「drink」是名詞，指「飲料」或「一杯喝的東西」，不是動詞「喝」。
generatedExample: Would you like a drink of water?

explanationZhTw: `gent` 是 `gentleman` 的口語縮略說法，意思是「紳士」或帶有稱讚意味的「好人／夠意思的人」。這裡是比較隨口、帶點階級或老派英式口氣的稱呼。
generatedExample: Thanks for helping me carry that box, you're a real gent.

explanationZhTw: `wallop` 在這裡不是一般的「猛擊」意思，而是俚語，指「啤酒」或「含酒精飲料」。整個片語 `pint of wallop` 就是「一品脫啤酒」。
generatedExample: After work, he ordered a pint of wallop at the pub.

explanationZhTw: 酒吧侍者，指在酒吧裡調酒、倒酒的人。
generatedExample: The barman smiled and poured me a cold drink.

explanationZhTw: 使液體快速流動或發出刷刷聲地倒入，帶有迅速、俐落的動作感。
generatedExample: She swished the water around in the bowl before pouring it out.

explanationZhTw: 沖洗；用清水快速洗掉表面的東西。
generatedExample: He rinsed the cup before filling it again.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是語境用法，指「無產階級／平民常去的酒吧」；`prole` 是 `proletarian` 的縮略口語。
generatedExample: We stopped at a prole pub after work for a cheap pint.

explanationZhTw: `be supposed to` 表示「應該／被規定要」；這裡是否定形式，表示規定不准喝琴酒。
generatedExample: Students are supposed not to use phones during the exam.

explanationZhTw: 實際上；理論或規定和真實情況相反時使用。
generatedExample: In practice, the new rule is hard to enforce.

explanationZhTw: 拿到、弄到；常表示設法取得某物，帶有「費點力氣也弄到」的語感。
generatedExample: I managed to get hold of her new phone number.

explanationZhTw: 熱烈進行中；形容活動已經非常活躍、進入最旺盛的階段。
generatedExample: By noon, the festival was in full swing.

explanationZhTw: 一小群人；`knot` 字面是「結」，這裡形容人群聚成一團。
generatedExample: A knot of students waited outside the classroom.

explanationZhTw: 暫時地；很短的一段時間。
generatedExample: For a moment, I thought I had lost my keys.

explanationZhTw: 被偷聽到；指談話被別人無意或暗中聽見。
generatedExample: We spoke quietly so we wouldn't be overheard.

explanationZhTw: 無論如何；用來把話題拉回重點，或表示前面不管怎樣，後面這點成立。
generatedExample: The plan is risky, but at any rate it is possible.

explanationZhTw: 方言／口語用法，這裡是「替我倒來／送來一品脫啤酒」的意思。
generatedExample: Could you draw me off a pint while I find a seat?

explanationZhTw: 指「抱怨、嘟囔」；這裡描寫老人不滿意飲料份量和價錢。
generatedExample: He grumbled about the cold coffee.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，這裡是「坐下並安頓好」的意思。
generatedExample: We settled down in the waiting room for an hour.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是抽象的「滿足」，而是指「喝了之後還是不夠、不夠過癮」。
generatedExample: One slice of bread doesn't satisfy me before lunch.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是委婉／口語說法，意思是「會讓我想上廁所、想尿尿」。
generatedExample: That much tea starts my bladder running on long trips.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「更不用說／何況」。
generatedExample: I can barely afford rent, let alone a new car.

explanationZhTw: 「tentatively」在這裡不是指「暫時地」，而是指說話時帶有猶豫、試探性的語氣；中文可理解為「試探性地、帶點遲疑地」說。
generatedExample: She asked tentatively whether the meeting could be postponed.

explanationZhTw: 「Gents」是英式口語，指男廁或男士洗手間。這裡不是一般的「先生們」，而是特定地點名稱。
generatedExample: I asked the waiter where the Gents were before going back to my table.

explanationZhTw: 「bar-room」指酒吧裡有吧檯的那個房間或區域，屬於較舊式、帶有場景感的說法。
generatedExample: They stayed in the bar-room until the rain stopped.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「beer-wallop」是老式口語，指一種便宜、普通的啤酒；不是字面上的「啤酒重擊」，而是帶點戲謔味的說法。
generatedExample: Back then, the pub served a weak beer-wallop that everyone drank after work.

explanationZhTw: 「fourpence」是舊式英國貨幣說法，表示四便士；這種歷史性金額在現代英文裡不常見。
generatedExample: In my grandfather's day, a loaf of bread might have cost fourpence.

explanationZhTw: 「war」是名詞，意思是「戰爭」。在這句裡，Winston 在問對方指的是哪一場戰爭。
generatedExample: People in the town were worried that the war might spread.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是副詞，表示「含糊地、語焉不詳地」。
generatedExample: She answered vaguely because she did not know the details.

explanationZhTw: 這是片語動詞，意思是「拿起、舉起」。
generatedExample: He took up the notebook and wrote the address down.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 glass 指「玻璃杯、酒杯」，不是玻璃材料。
generatedExample: He raised his glass and smiled at the guests.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「身體重新挺直／姿勢恢復筆直」，不是單純整理物品。
generatedExample: When she heard the news, her back straightened again.

explanationZhTw: 這是帶口音的寫法，''Ere's = here's，'ealth = health，整句是在說「祝你身體健康」。
generatedExample: Here's wishing you the very best of health on your birthday.

explanationZhTw: 喉結；指喉嚨前方那個明顯突出的部位。這裡是描述外觀的名詞片語。
generatedExample: His Adam's apple moved when he swallowed.

explanationZhTw: 上下移動；這裡用來形容喉結快速起伏的動作。
generatedExample: The needle showed a small up-and-down movement.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是魔法式的「消失」，而是指啤酒很快就被喝完、一下子沒了。
generatedExample: The water vanished in a few seconds.

explanationZhTw: 帶著……回來；這裡是指文斯頓去吧台拿了兩杯酒回來。
generatedExample: She came back with a bottle of water.

explanationZhTw: 對……的偏見／成見。這裡表示老人原本不喜歡喝整整一公升的酒。
generatedExample: He had a prejudice against spicy food.

explanationZhTw: 「the old days」指以前、過去的時代；這裡不是字面上的「老日子」，而是「從前、舊時光」。
generatedExample: She sometimes talks about the old days when everyone knew each other.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「the Revolution」是特定歷史事件的指稱，意思是改變政權或社會制度的那場革命；屬於語境中特定的政治／歷史用法。
generatedExample: After the Revolution, the country changed very quickly.

explanationZhTw: 「oppression」指壓迫、受壓制的狀態，常用來描述權力對人民的不公平控制。
generatedExample: The book describes years of oppression under a harsh government.

explanationZhTw: 「injustice」指不公正、不公平的待遇；這裡和 oppression、poverty 並列，強調社會問題很嚴重。
generatedExample: Many people protested against the injustice in the court system.

explanationZhTw: 「poverty」指貧窮、缺乏生活資源的狀態。
generatedExample: Poverty made it hard for the family to buy food and clothes.

explanationZhTw: 「capitalists」指資本家；在這裡是特定社會階級的稱呼，不只是一般的「有錢人」。
generatedExample: The capitalists owned most of the factories in the city.

explanationZhTw: 「gorgeous」在這裡表示非常華麗、豪華，帶有強烈的視覺與富裕感。
generatedExample: They stayed in a gorgeous hotel by the sea.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡，`brightened` 不是字面上的「變亮」，而是指「神情變得開朗、精神振作起來」。這是常見的情境用法。
generatedExample: Her face brightened when she heard the good news.

explanationZhTw: 指「禮帽／高帽子」，這裡是具體服飾名稱。
generatedExample: He took off his top hat before entering the room.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，意思是「你剛好提到它們真巧／說到這個正好」。
generatedExample: Funny you should mention it, because I was just thinking about the same thing.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是方言式說法，意思是「已經完全過時、沒人再用了」。
generatedExample: That style has gone right out of fashion.

explanationZhTw: 指「姊夫／妹夫的家人中的配偶」，也就是「姻親的姊妹／兄弟的太太或丈夫」；此處是「妻子的姊妹／兄弟的配偶」這類親屬稱呼。
generatedExample: My sister-in-law is visiting us this weekend.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是方言式寫法，意思是「租來的／借來的（暫時使用）」。
generatedExample: They hired a car for the trip.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「為了這個特定場合／臨時應景地」。
generatedExample: She bought a dress for the occasion.

explanationZhTw: 指「資本家」這個社會階層；在這裡不是一般職業描述，而是帶有政治經濟批判意味的用法。
generatedExample: The capitalists controlled most of the city's factories.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「大地的主人」，實際上是誇張地指握有極大權力、支配他人的人。
generatedExample: In that decade, the media tycoons seemed like the lords of the earth.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，這裡是「把某人打發、送走、送往別處」，帶有強迫或不太尊重的語氣。
generatedExample: The company might ship you off to another branch next month.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是真的指牛群，而是把人貶低成像牲畜一樣，強調被粗暴對待。
generatedExample: The refugees were treated like cattle at the checkpoint.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「鞭打、抽打」，不是一般的「推銷」或「賣力宣傳」那種 flog 用法，而是嚴厲體罰。
generatedExample: The guards threatened to have the prisoners flogged.

explanationZhTw: 一種舊式刑具，多股鞭子，常用來施以鞭刑；屬於歷史性詞彙。
generatedExample: The pirate in the story was punished with a cat-o'-nine tails.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「脫帽致敬」，是表示尊敬的社會習慣，不只是單純拿下帽子。
generatedExample: He took his cap off when he entered the church.

explanationZhTw: 指「狗腿子、跟班、奴才」，帶有貶義，表示替有權勢者跑腿的人。
generatedExample: The minister was always surrounded by a few loyal lackeys.

explanationZhTw: `brightened` 在這裡不是指「變亮」，而是指「臉色/神情變得開朗、精神一振」。屬於依上下文才看得出的意思。
generatedExample: When she heard the good news, her face brightened at once.

explanationZhTw: 「donkey's years」是很口語的說法，表示「很久很久以前／好幾年沒發生」；這裡是在回憶很久以前的事。
generatedExample: I haven't seen that shop in donkey's years.

explanationZhTw: 「blokes」是英式口語，指「男人、傢伙」。這裡不是正式用語，而是很口語的說法。
generatedExample: A couple of blokes were chatting by the bus stop.

explanationZhTw: 這是很口語的強調說法，意思是「他狠狠地罵了他們／把他們痛批一頓」，語氣很重。
generatedExample: When the report came out, the minister didn't 'alf give it us.

explanationZhTw: 「bourgeoisie」在政治或社會語境裡指「資產階級、富裕上層階級」。這裡帶有明顯批判意味。
generatedExample: The speaker accused the bourgeoisie of exploiting workers.

explanationZhTw: 這裡把人比作「hyenas（鬣狗）」，是帶貶義的隱喻，用來形容貪婪、兇狠或令人厭惡的人。
generatedExample: He called the corrupt officials hyenas for feeding on the weak.

explanationZhTw: 「at cross-purposes」表示雙方理解不同、彼此誤會，說話沒有對上焦點，像是在各講各的。這裡不是字面上的「在相反目的上」，而是指溝通失焦、彼此沒有真正對話。
generatedExample: We kept talking at cross-purposes until she finally explained what she meant.

explanationZhTw: 「more freedom」指「更多自由、較少受限制」。這裡是在比較現在和過去的自由程度。
generatedExample: After moving to college, she felt she had more freedom to choose her own schedule.

explanationZhTw: 「treated more like a human being」不是單純字面上的「像人類一樣對待」，而是指「被當人看、受到基本尊重與公平對待」。
generatedExample: At the new job, employees are treated more like a human being and less like numbers.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是英國上議院 `House of Lords`，但作者故意寫成 `'Ouse`，表示口語或地方腔把字首 `h` 吞掉了。這不是一般字面拼法，而是用來表現說話人的口音與語氣。
generatedExample: He said it like an old Londoner, dropping the `h` in `house`.

explanationZhTw: 英國議會的上議院；這裡是在提一個英國社會階級很高的機構，帶有明顯的文化背景。
generatedExample: He gave a speech in the House of Lords last week.

explanationZhTw: 表示「如果你要這麼說／如果你願意這樣稱呼」；語氣上是在讓步或舉例，不是字面上的「如果你喜歡」。
generatedExample: We can call it a meeting, if you like.

explanationZhTw: 把某人當成地位較低、較不重要的人對待；這裡的 inferior 是指「地位較低的人」。
generatedExample: Nobody should treat you as an inferior at work.

explanationZhTw: 表示「只是因為／單單因為」；用來強調原因很單一，帶有批評或不滿的語氣。
generatedExample: She was ignored simply because she was new.

explanationZhTw: 表示「例如」；用來引出一個具體例子。
generatedExample: We need better habits, for instance walking more every day.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，表示「脫下、取下」；這裡是把帽子脫下來。
generatedExample: Please take off your shoes before you enter.

explanationZhTw: 「drank off」是片語動詞，意思是把飲料一下子喝掉、快速喝完。這裡不是單純的「喝」，而是帶有「把手上的酒喝掉一部分」的動作感。
generatedExample: He drank off the last of his coffee and stood up to leave.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，意思是「脫帽/碰帽致意」，表示對對方行禮、打招呼或表達敬意。
generatedExample: He touched his cap to the old woman as he passed.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 respect 是「尊敬、禮貌對待」，整個片語是在說這個動作代表禮貌和敬意。
generatedExample: In our family, standing up when elders enter showed respect.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是口語省略，完整意思是「不得不這麼做」；在上下文中表示這不是自願，而是被迫要做。
generatedExample: He had to leave early because of work.

explanationZhTw: 「usual」這裡是「常見的、慣常的」意思，指這種事是不是平常會發生。
generatedExample: Was it usual for people in that town to leave their doors unlocked?

explanationZhTw: 「usual」這裡是「常見的、慣常的」意思，指這種事是不是平常會發生。
generatedExample: Was it usual for people in that town to leave their doors unlocked?

explanationZhTw: 「push you off」是片語動詞，意思是把某人推開、逼離原本的位置。
generatedExample: Two guards pushed him off the platform before the train arrived.

explanationZhTw: 「the pavement」在英式英文是「人行道」；美式英文通常會說 sidewalk。
generatedExample: She stopped at the pavement and waited for the light to change.

explanationZhTw: 「the gutter」原本是路邊排水溝；在這裡表示被推到很低、很髒的位置，帶有貶抑感。
generatedExample: After the storm, leaves collected in the gutter outside the house.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `rowdy` 指「吵鬧、失序、鬧哄哄的」，常用來形容酒後或人多時的混亂場面。
generatedExample: The pub got rowdy after the football match.

explanationZhTw: `bump into` 在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「不小心撞到」或「偶然遇見」。
generatedExample: I bumped into an old classmate at the station.

explanationZhTw: `gent` 是 `gentleman` 的口語縮寫，這裡指「看起來很體面、很像紳士的男人」。
generatedExample: He looked like a real gent in his suit.

explanationZhTw: `zig-zagging` 指「之字形地前進、左右搖擺地走」，強調路線不直。
generatedExample: The cyclist was zig-zagging between the parked cars.

explanationZhTw: `accidental-like` 是口語說法，意思接近「不小心地、意外地」。這種 `-like` 用法比較非正式。
generatedExample: I called her accidental-like while I was scrolling through my contacts.

explanationZhTw: 這是帶挑釁的反問，意思是「你以為你把人行道買下來了嗎？」；暗示對方太囂張、擋路。
generatedExample: Move over, mate. You haven't bought the bleeding pavement.

explanationZhTw: `get fresh with someone` 是慣用語，意思是「對某人無禮、冒犯、耍嘴皮子」。
generatedExample: Don't get fresh with me just because you're angry.

explanationZhTw: `give someone in charge` 是英式口語，意思是「把某人交給警察、報警抓人」。
generatedExample: If you keep shouting, I'll give you in charge.

explanationZhTw: `fetch someone one` 是口語，意思是「揍某人一拳」或「給某人一下」。
generatedExample: He was so angry he nearly fetched the man one.

explanationZhTw: 「took hold」在這裡指「開始強烈支配、緊緊抓住」某人的情緒或狀態，不是字面上的「拿住」。
generatedExample: A panic took hold of her when she saw the empty platform.

explanationZhTw: 「rubbish-heap」這裡是比喻用法，指一堆雜亂、沒有條理、幾乎無法整理的細節。
generatedExample: His notes had become a rubbish-heap of half-finished ideas.

explanationZhTw: 「after a fashion」是慣用語，表示「勉強算是、某種程度上、但不完全如此」。
generatedExample: The plan worked after a fashion, but it still needed a lot of fixing.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「把意思說清楚、表達清楚」。
generatedExample: Let me make myself clear: I want the blue one, not the red one.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指特定歷史背景中的「革命／政權更替」，不是一般抽象的改變。
generatedExample: My grandparents remembered life before the Revolution.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「例如」的意思，用來舉例說明。
generatedExample: Some fruits, for instance apples and bananas, are easy to pack.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「長大」的動作，而是指「已成年、是大人」。
generatedExample: She was grown up enough to make her own decisions.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「較喜歡、較想要」的意思。
generatedExample: I prefer tea to coffee in the morning.

explanationZhTw: 副詞，表示「沉思地、若有所思地」。這裡是在描寫老人安靜、專注地看著飛鏢板。
generatedExample: She stared meditatively out of the window before answering.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，表示「把剩下的喝完／吃完」。這裡是指他把啤酒喝完。
generatedExample: I finished up my coffee and left the cafe.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 philosophical 不是「哲學的」學科意思，而是指「冷靜、泰然、能接受事情的態度」。
generatedExample: He answered with a philosophical air, as if the delay did not bother him.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「使他變得更溫和、更放鬆」。用在啤酒上，是說酒讓他的態度沒那麼尖銳了。
generatedExample: A few minutes in the sun mellowed her mood.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `I'd sooner` 不是字面上的「更快」，而是口語的「我寧願／我比較想」。
generatedExample: I'd sooner walk than wait for the bus.

explanationZhTw: 這是方言拼法，等於 `ask`，意思是「問」。
generatedExample: If you arst her politely, she may say yes.

explanationZhTw: `my time of life` 是慣用說法，指「我這個年紀／我這把年紀」。
generatedExample: At my time of life, I prefer quiet evenings.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `wicked` 不是「邪惡」，而是口語用法，表示「非常、很嚴重地」。
generatedExample: My back hurts something wicked after the hike.

explanationZhTw: `No truck with women` 是慣用說法，意思是「不跟女人來往／沒有女人方面的牽扯」。
generatedExample: He wants no truck with gossip at work.

explanationZhTw: `if you'd credit it` 的意思是「如果你相信得了的話／信不信由你」。
generatedExample: He solved the whole issue in ten minutes, if you'd credit it.

explanationZhTw: 表示「沒有用、徒勞」；這裡指繼續下去也沒有意義。
generatedExample: There is no use arguing with him after he has made up his mind.

explanationZhTw: 表示某物開始對某人產生影響；這裡是酒精開始影響他。
generatedExample: The medicine is working on her, and she feels less pain now.

explanationZhTw: 表示「至多、最多」；強調時間或數量的上限。
generatedExample: It will take two hours at the most to finish the report.

explanationZhTw: 表示「徹底地、一次解決後不再反覆」；這裡指問題終究會完全無法回答。
generatedExample: We need to settle this once and for all.

explanationZhTw: 表示「實際上、事實上」；用來指出真實狀況與表面說法不同。
generatedExample: The new rule is, in effect, a ban on late submissions.

explanationZhTw: 「train of thought」指一連串的思緒、思路；這裡表示他的思緒被突然打斷了。
generatedExample: I lost my train of thought when the phone rang.

explanationZhTw: 「halted」是「停下來、停止前進」；這裡指他突然站住不動。
generatedExample: The runner halted at the red light.

explanationZhTw: 「looked up」在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「抬頭看」。
generatedExample: She looked up when she heard her name.

explanationZhTw: 「interspersed」表示「散布在……之間、夾雜在……中」；這裡是說幾間店舖零散地夾在住家之間。
generatedExample: Small parks were interspersed among the office buildings.

explanationZhTw: 「gilded」原指「鍍金的、貼金箔的」；這裡表示那些金屬球以前可能曾經覆上一層金色。
generatedExample: The frame was gilded to make it look more elegant.

explanationZhTw: 「junk-shop」是「舊貨店、雜貨舖、跳蚤店」；這裡指他買日記本的那家二手雜物店。
generatedExample: I found an old camera in a junk-shop downtown.

explanationZhTw: 「twinge」原本是「一陣刺痛」；這裡指突然冒出的一小陣恐懼感。
generatedExample: She felt a twinge of fear before opening the door.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「穿過他」，而是指恐懼感突然湧上心頭、讓他整個人受到影響。
generatedExample: A wave of excitement went through him when he heard the news.

explanationZhTw: 「rash」表示魯莽、欠考慮；整個片語指一個太衝動、沒有深思熟慮的行動。
generatedExample: It was a rash act to quit without a plan.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，表示「自然而然地、出於本能地」，不是有人刻意控制。
generatedExample: The words came to her of their own accord.

explanationZhTw: 「impulses」是衝動、念頭；這裡指帶有自毀傾向的衝動。
generatedExample: He was struggling to control his suicidal impulses.

explanationZhTw: 指「顯眼的、容易被注意到的」；這裡是在說他待在室內比較不會引人注意。
generatedExample: Her bright coat made her conspicuous in the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是口語用法，表示「閒晃、逗留、在附近無所事事地待著」。
generatedExample: We were hanging about outside the station after school.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「看似合理」，而是指他能提出一個聽起來合理、足以應付質問的說法。
generatedExample: She could plausibly claim that she missed the train because of traffic.

explanationZhTw: 店主；較正式的說法，指某個場所或生意的擁有人。
generatedExample: The proprietor of the small bookshop knew every regular customer by name.

explanationZhTw: 散發出；這裡是片語動詞，表示發出氣味。
generatedExample: The old machine gave off a loud buzzing sound.

explanationZhTw: 虛弱的；體力不強、看起來很脆弱。
generatedExample: After the illness, he looked frail and tired.

explanationZhTw: 彎腰的、佝僂的；這裡形容身體前傾、姿勢彎曲。
generatedExample: The bowed old man walked slowly down the street.

explanationZhTw: 慈祥的、善意的；帶有友善而溫和的感覺。
generatedExample: She gave me a benevolent smile before leaving.

explanationZhTw: 扭曲的；這裡指眼鏡讓眼睛看起來變形。
generatedExample: The cheap mirror distorted her face.

explanationZhTw: 眼鏡；較正式或老派的說法。
generatedExample: He removed his spectacles and cleaned the lenses.

explanationZhTw: 講究細節的、過度挑剔的；也可形容動作很拘謹、繁瑣。
generatedExample: My father is fussy about how the table is set.

explanationZhTw: 知識分子氣質、書卷氣；較抽象，指看起來像受過良好教育的人。
generatedExample: The professor's calm speech gave the room a quiet intellectuality.

explanationZhTw: 墮落的、劣化的；這裡指口音沒有那麼粗鄙或退化。
generatedExample: The novel warns against a debased version of the original idea.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的「無產者」；在小說裡指普通平民階層。
generatedExample: The proles in the story have little political power.

explanationZhTw: 英式英文的「人行道、路邊」。
generatedExample: I dropped my keys on the pavement outside the station.

explanationZhTw: 「紳士」；這裡是較客氣、正式的稱呼。
generatedExample: The gentleman at the door asked if we had an appointment.

explanationZhTw: 「紀念冊／留念冊」；用來保存簽名、照片或留言。
generatedExample: She kept all the wedding photos in a keepsake album.

explanationZhTw: 紙張規格名，指質地細緻、偏高級的紙。
generatedExample: The invitation was printed on cream-laid paper.

explanationZhTw: 「仔細看、眯著眼看」；比一般的看更刻意。
generatedExample: She peered through the window to see who was outside.

explanationZhTw: 較正式的「眼鏡」。
generatedExample: He cleaned his spectacles before reading the letter.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，「四處看看、參觀一下」。
generatedExample: We arrived early and had a quick look round the museum.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「傳遞」的意思，而是「路過、經過」。
generatedExample: I was passing your office, so I stopped to say hello.

explanationZhTw: 表示「含糊地、不明確地」，也就是說話或表達得不清楚。
generatedExample: She vaguely remembered meeting him at a conference.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `look in` 是片語動詞，意思是「順道進來、順便來訪」。
generatedExample: I looked in on my grandmother after work.

explanationZhTw: `in particular` 在這裡表示「特別是、某個特定的」，`anything in particular` 就是「任何特定的東西」。
generatedExample: Do you need anything in particular from the store?

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「幸好；也無妨」，意思是前面那件事反而是好事，或至少沒有造成問題。
generatedExample: It's just as well we arrived early, because the seats were almost gone.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「假設」，而是委婉地表示「我想大概不會」或「我覺得不太可能」。
generatedExample: I don't suppose he will call back before noon.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「你知我知就好；別外傳」，通常是在說秘密或私下看法。
generatedExample: Between you and me, I think the plan will change again.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 trade 指「行業；生意」，不是單次交易；antique trade 就是「古董業」。
generatedExample: The book trade has changed a lot in the last ten years.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「存貨；庫存」，不是股票。
generatedExample: The small shop has almost no stock left today.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，這裡表示「被拆碎、打散」，也就是東西被逐步拆掉或弄成碎片。
generatedExample: They broke up the old table and used the wood for firewood.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，指「熔化成液體」，常用來表示金屬被熔掉以便回收再利用。
generatedExample: The old coins were melted down and turned into jewelry.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `uncomfortably full` 不是單純「滿」，而是指空間擁擠到讓人覺得不舒服。
generatedExample: The train was uncomfortably full, so I had to stand the whole way.

explanationZhTw: `floorspace` 指地面可用的空間，特別常用在房間、店面或建築的描述。
generatedExample: The new display takes up less floorspace than the old one.

explanationZhTw: `innumerable` 表示「數不清的、無數的」，語氣比 `many` 更強。
generatedExample: There were innumerable reasons to leave, but I stayed.

explanationZhTw: `tarnished` 原本是指金屬表面失去光澤；這裡形容手錶看起來陳舊、失去原本的亮度。
generatedExample: She wore a tarnished silver ring from her grandmother.

explanationZhTw: `in going order` 是較古老或文學性的說法，意思是「能正常運作、還可使用」。
generatedExample: The old radio was not in going order, so we threw it away.

explanationZhTw: `odds and ends` 是慣用語，指零碎雜物、各式各樣不成套的小東西。
generatedExample: I found a box of odds and ends in the attic.

explanationZhTw: `his eye was caught by` 是慣用表達，表示「他的目光被某物吸引住了」。
generatedExample: My eye was caught by a poster in the station.

explanationZhTw: `picked it up` 在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「把它撿起來、拿起來」。
generatedExample: He picked it up and turned it over in his hands.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 lump of glass 指「一大塊玻璃」或「團狀玻璃」，不是別的引申義。
generatedExample: The paperweight was just a lump of glass, but it caught the light beautifully.

explanationZhTw: hemisphere 是「半球」的意思；這裡是說這塊玻璃的形狀幾乎像半個球體。
generatedExample: The model showed the northern hemisphere of the Earth.

explanationZhTw: peculiar 在這裡是「奇特的、異樣的」；peculiar softness 就是「一種很特別的柔和感」。
generatedExample: There was a peculiar softness in her voice that made everyone listen.

explanationZhTw: 這是固定說法，意思是「在核心／中心處」；不是字面上的「心臟」。
generatedExample: At the heart of it, the problem was a lack of communication.

explanationZhTw: magnified 在這裡是「被放大」的意思，指曲面讓中央的東西看起來更大。
generatedExample: The text looked magnified through the old lens.

explanationZhTw: convoluted 形容「彎曲盤繞、結構複雜」；這裡是在描寫那個物體的形狀。
generatedExample: The sculpture had a convoluted shape that was hard to describe.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 recalled 不是「回想起」，而是「讓人聯想到／看起來像」。
generatedExample: The melody recalled a song I had heard as a child.

explanationZhTw: sea anemone 指「海葵」，是一種海洋生物。
generatedExample: A bright sea anemone clung to the rock near the tide pool.

explanationZhTw: 「fascinated」表示「深深著迷／極感興趣」，用來形容人對某件事非常投入、想知道更多。這裡不是單純的「喜歡」，而是帶有強烈好奇與注意力被完全吸引的語氣。
generatedExample: She was fascinated by the old map and kept studying every detail.

explanationZhTw: `used to` 表示「過去常常／以前習慣會」，強調現在不再如此。
generatedExample: We used to visit this market every Sunday.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `embed` 不是一般「嵌入文件」的意思，而是指把東西牢牢鑲進或封進另一種材料裡。
generatedExample: The artist embedded tiny stones in the floor of the entrance.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `less than` 放在否定句裡，語氣上等於「至少有」或「不可能少於」。整句是在說這東西做成的時間很久了。
generatedExample: That car wasn't built less than twenty years ago.

explanationZhTw: `by the look of it` 是慣用語，意思是「從外表看來、看樣子」。
generatedExample: By the look of it, we will need another hour to finish.

explanationZhTw: 「beautiful」是常見的 A2 字彙，意思是「美麗的、漂亮的」，用來形容外觀或感受上令人愉悅的事物。
generatedExample: The sunset was beautiful.

explanationZhTw: 表示「帶著欣賞、讚許的態度」；這裡是在稱讚那件東西很美。
generatedExample: She nodded appreciatively at the careful design.

explanationZhTw: 表示「如今、現代」；用來對比過去和現在。
generatedExample: Nowadays, many people read books on their phones.

explanationZhTw: 比較正式的說法，意思是「如果碰巧／萬一」；帶有假設、非固定安排的語氣。
generatedExample: If it so happened that the store was closed, we would come back tomorrow.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「去拿」，而是指「賣到／值到（某個價錢）」；是價格語境下的特殊用法。
generatedExample: That old chair fetched a surprisingly high price at the auction.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是「算出來、理解清楚」；這裡表示說話者算不出八英鎊到底相當於多少。
generatedExample: I can't work it out unless you show me the steps.

explanationZhTw: 這是反問式說法，意思接近「誰還在乎／反正沒人在意」；語氣帶有輕蔑或無所謂。
generatedExample: Who cares about the label if the jacket is warm?

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示把錢付出、交給對方；語氣比單純 `paid` 更像「把錢交付出去」。
generatedExample: She paid over the fee before entering the museum.

explanationZhTw: `coveted` 表示「很多人渴望得到的、令人垂涎的」，帶有強烈想要的意思。
generatedExample: That coveted job offer went to someone with more experience.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `air` 不是「空氣」，而是「氛圍、感覺、神態」。
generatedExample: There was an air of mystery around the old house.

explanationZhTw: 這個字在這裡用來形容玻璃像雨水一樣清淡、朦朧，帶點特別的視覺印象。
generatedExample: The rainwatery light made the room feel unreal.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `queer` 是舊式用法，表示「奇怪的、古怪的」。
generatedExample: It felt queer to hear no sound in the busy station.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `compromising` 不是「妥協的」，而是「可能讓人抓到把柄、對名聲或立場不利的」。
generatedExample: Keeping those emails was a compromising choice for the official.

explanationZhTw: 表示「在他手上、由他持有」，是較正式的說法。
generatedExample: The stolen phone was found in her possession.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `suspect` 是形容詞，表示「可疑的、讓人覺得不太對勁的」，不是動詞「懷疑」。
generatedExample: His story sounded vaguely suspect to the investigator.

explanationZhTw: `take a look at` 是常見口語說法，意思是「看一下、檢查一下」。這裡是禮貌地邀請對方去看看那個房間。
generatedExample: Could you take a look at this report before lunch?

explanationZhTw: 這裡 `do with` 不是字面上的「做與……有關的事」，而是指「需要、得靠……才行」或「可以用得上……」。
generatedExample: We can do with an extra blanket tonight.

explanationZhTw: 「lit」是「light」的過去式，這裡指「點亮」燈。
generatedExample: She lit a candle before going to bed.

explanationZhTw: 「steep」在這裡是「陡的」，形容樓梯坡度很大。
generatedExample: The path is steep, so walk carefully.

explanationZhTw: 「worn」表示「磨損的、舊而使用痕跡明顯的」。
generatedExample: His shoes were old and worn after years of use.

explanationZhTw: 「passage」這裡是「通道、走廊」的意思，不是一般的「段落」。
generatedExample: We walked through a narrow passage to reach the kitchen.

explanationZhTw: 「give on」在這裡表示「朝向、通到」，常用來說建築物的窗或門面向哪裡。
generatedExample: The hotel room gives on to a quiet garden.

explanationZhTw: 「cobbled」指「鋪有鵝卵石的」，用來形容地面。
generatedExample: They crossed a cobbled street outside the old church.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻說法，表示屋頂上有很多煙囪，看起來像一片森林。
generatedExample: From the hill, we saw a forest of radio towers in the city.

explanationZhTw: 「arranged」指「擺放、排列」，這裡說家具還保留著像有人居住時的樣子。
generatedExample: The books were arranged by color on the shelf.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「本來就是要讓人住的」，強調房間看起來仍像可供居住。
generatedExample: The apartment felt warm and meant to be lived in.

explanationZhTw: 「slatternly」形容「邋遢的、凌亂不整潔的」。
generatedExample: The room had a slatternly look, with clothes everywhere.

explanationZhTw: 「drawn up」在這裡是「拉近、移到靠近某處」的意思。
generatedExample: She drew up a chair next to the bed.

explanationZhTw: 「mantelpiece」是「壁爐架、壁爐上方的架子」。
generatedExample: There were family photos on the mantelpiece.

explanationZhTw: 「occupying」這裡是「佔據（空間）」的意思。
generatedExample: The table is occupying most of the small kitchen.

explanationZhTw: 「enormous」表示「巨大的、非常大的」。
generatedExample: They live in an enormous house near the river.

explanationZhTw: 「mattress」是「床墊」。
generatedExample: We bought a new mattress for the guest room.

explanationZhTw: 「歉疚地、帶著歉意地」；表示說話時的語氣有點不好意思。
generatedExample: He apologetically said he had forgotten the meeting.

explanationZhTw: `sell off` 指「逐步把東西賣掉」；常用來表示清掉存貨或處理不需要的物品。
generatedExample: They sold off the old office chairs after the move.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「一點一點地、逐漸地」；強調動作是慢慢進行的。
generatedExample: Little by little, she saved enough money for a new laptop.

explanationZhTw: 「桃花心木」；一種深紅褐色、常見於高級家具的木材。
generatedExample: The desk was made of mahogany and looked very old.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「笨重」，而是指「搬動或使用起來很不方便、很麻煩」。
generatedExample: The old machine is cumbersome, so we replaced it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `inviting` 不是「邀請」的意思，而是「誘人、吸引人、讓人想待在那裡」的意思。
generatedExample: The small cafe looked inviting on a cold rainy day.

explanationZhTw: `flit through` 在這裡表示「一閃而過、快速掠過」。整個片語常用來描述念頭、影像或情緒短暫出現在腦中。
generatedExample: The idea flitted through my mind for a second, then vanished.

explanationZhTw: `notion` 指「念頭、想法」，比 `idea` 更帶有主觀、模糊，甚至有點不切實際的感覺。
generatedExample: She had the notion that the train would be late.

explanationZhTw: `nostalgia` 是「懷舊、對過去的思念」。通常指想到以前的時光而產生的溫暖或感傷情緒。
generatedExample: That old song filled him with nostalgia.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是文學性的說法，意思接近「彷彿從祖先傳下來的深層記憶或本能」，不是字面上真的記得祖先的事。
generatedExample: The landscape stirred some ancestral memory in her.

explanationZhTw: `fender` 在這裡是壁爐前的金屬擋板或護板；放腳在 `the fender` 上，就是坐在火爐旁取暖。
generatedExample: He sat with his feet on the fender and read the paper.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的虛構裝置，結合電視與監控螢幕的概念，用來播放內容並監視人。
generatedExample: The citizens knew the telescreen could watch them at any moment.

explanationZhTw: 「忍不住低聲說」；`could not help` 表示無法控制自己不去做某事，整個片語是固定用法。
generatedExample: She could not help smiling when she heard the news.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是口語性的泛指說法，意思是「那種東西／那類用品」。說話者表示自己從來沒有這種物件。
generatedExample: I don't own one of those things, so I usually borrow mine from a neighbor.

explanationZhTw: 摺腳桌；桌面兩側可翻開、桌腳可摺疊收放的一種桌子。
generatedExample: We put a small gateleg table in the kitchen for extra space when guests come over.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指桌子可翻開的桌板或桌翼，不是一般最常見的「拍打、拍翅」意思。
generatedExample: The flaps fold down neatly when the table is not in use.

explanationZhTw: 自然地靠向；這裡表示溫斯頓幾乎是本能地走近那個書櫃。
generatedExample: She gravitated towards the window when the meeting started.

explanationZhTw: 垃圾、廢物；這裡指裡面只有沒用的雜物或廢棄物。
generatedExample: We cleared out the garage and threw away all the rubbish.

explanationZhTw: 追捕、搜查；這裡指有系統地追查並找出書本來銷毀。
generatedExample: The police kept hunting down the source of the leak.

explanationZhTw: 徹底、周密；這裡強調做事非常完整、沒有留下任何漏網之處。
generatedExample: Her thoroughness made the report accurate and reliable.

explanationZhTw: 「happen to」在這裡不是指「發生」，而是帶有「如果剛好／碰巧」的語氣，用來把提議說得更委婉。
generatedExample: If you happen to be free tomorrow, we could visit the museum.

explanationZhTw: 「prints」在這裡是指印刷品、版畫或老式印刷圖片，不是一般動詞「列印」。整個片語指「古老的印刷品／版畫」。
generatedExample: She collects old prints from the nineteenth century.

explanationZhTw: 「at all」在這裡用來加強語氣，意思接近「任何一點也好／無論如何都算」。
generatedExample: I don't mind helping at all if you need me.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「delicately」表示說話方式很小心、委婉、帶點試探性，不是單純的「脆弱地」。
generatedExample: He asked delicately whether she wanted to leave early.

explanationZhTw: 「came across」在這裡不是「偶然遇到」，而是「走過去、走近」的意思。
generatedExample: She came across to check the map on the wall.

explanationZhTw: 「examine」指「仔細查看、檢視」，比單純的「看」更正式、更用力。
generatedExample: The doctor examined the patient carefully.

explanationZhTw: 「rear end」在這裡是建築物的「後端／後方」，屬於依上下文才看得出的意思。
generatedExample: The entrance is at the rear end of the building.

explanationZhTw: 「gazed」指「凝視、久久看著」，帶有目光停留較久、較專注的語氣。
generatedExample: She gazed at the mountains in silence.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `fixed` 不是「固定好」的抽象概念，而是指「牢牢裝在、連在」牆上。
generatedExample: The shelf is fixed to the wall, so it will not move.

explanationZhTw: `unscrew` 指「把螺絲轉鬆、拆下來」，也就是用轉動的方式把東西從固定位置卸開。
generatedExample: Please unscrew the lid before you wash the bottle.

explanationZhTw: `I dare say` 在這裡是口語用法，表示「我想應該可以／大概可以」，帶有一點不那麼肯定但仍然合理的語氣。
generatedExample: I dare say she will arrive before dinner.

explanationZhTw: building 指「建築物、房子」。這裡是一般用法，屬於常見基礎字彙。
generatedExample: That building is older than it looks.

explanationZhTw: ruin 在這裡指「廢墟、殘破的建築物」，不是「毀滅」的動詞用法。
generatedExample: After the fire, the house was a ruin.

explanationZhTw: 「bombed」在這裡是被動用法，意思是「被炸毀、遭到轟炸」。這不是在說情緒很糟，而是描述建築物曾受戰火破壞。
generatedExample: The old bridge was bombed during the war.

explanationZhTw: 「at one time」表示「曾經、一度、以前」。這裡是在說這棟建築過去曾是教堂。
generatedExample: At one time, this building was a hospital.

explanationZhTw: 「apologetically」是「帶著歉意地、抱歉地」的意思，用來描述他微笑時的態度。
generatedExample: She smiled apologetically and left the room.

explanationZhTw: 「conscious of」在這裡是「意識到、知道自己正在做某事」的意思。整句是在說他似乎知道自己說了有點可笑的話。
generatedExample: He was conscious of the awkward silence.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，指押韻短詩、童謠或順口溜。這裡是指一首大家會背的兒歌。
generatedExample: We learned a rhyme about the days of the week in class.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞 go on 在這裡是『接著怎麼唱／怎麼繼續』，不是泛指事情繼續發生。
generatedExample: I know the first line, but I don't remember how the song goes on.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，表示最後變成、最後落到某個結果。這裡是說童謠最後收在那幾句。
generatedExample: We got lost and ended up arriving an hour late.

explanationZhTw: 口語中的弱化說法，接近『有點像／算是』。這裡不是嚴格定義的舞蹈，而是比較隨口的描述。
generatedExample: It was kind of a strange meeting, but it went well.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是伸出、張開。這裡指把手臂向外伸平。
generatedExample: She held out her hand to greet me.

explanationZhTw: principal 在這裡表示『主要的、最重要的』，不是『校長』。整個片語指最重要的那些教堂。
generatedExample: The principal ones to remember are safety, speed, and clarity.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指「屬於某人」，而是指「可追溯到／歸於某個年代」。
generatedExample: The bridge belonged to the early 1800s.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「判定、判斷」年代，不只是一般的「決定」。
generatedExample: We tried to determine the cause of the noise.

explanationZhTw: 指「把……歸因於／認定為」，語氣較正式。
generatedExample: The success was ascribed to careful planning.

explanationZhTw: 指「中世紀」，是固定的歷史時期名稱。
generatedExample: The castle was built in the Middle Ages.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意為「揭示、說明清楚」。
generatedExample: The diary may throw light upon what really happened.

explanationZhTw: 「church」指「教堂」，這裡是一般建築用途的名詞；在這句話中，它強調這棟建築以前曾經是教堂。
generatedExample: The old house used to be a church before it was converted into apartments.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「被改作別的用途／另行利用」。這裡不是字面上的「放到其他用途」，而是指原本的東西已經不再照原來方式使用。
generatedExample: These old boxes have been put to other uses as storage shelves.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「我想到／我記起來了」。這裡表示說話者突然想起剛剛在找的內容。
generatedExample: Wait, I’ve got it! The answer is on the last page.

explanationZhTw: 這裡把教堂鐘聲擬人化，像是在「說話」；在這首童謠裡是固定的語句，不是字面上的鐘真的會說話。
generatedExample: At noon, the bells of the old church seemed to announce the hour.

explanationZhTw: `farthing` 是英國舊時的小額硬幣，現在已不常用；這裡指很小的金額。
generatedExample: In the story, the boy found two farthings in his pocket.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是慣用說法，表示「我能做到的極限／只能到這裡」。不是字面上的「走到多遠」。
generatedExample: I fixed the first bug, but that's as far as I can get without the logs.

explanationZhTw: `farthing` 是英國舊時的小額銅幣，現在多半只會在歷史或文學語境看到。
generatedExample: In old stories, a farthing could buy very little.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是精確比較，而是表示「看起來有點像／大概像」。屬於語境中的近似說法。
generatedExample: The new badge looked something like the old one, but not exactly.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純指「站著」，而是指建築物「還存在、還沒被拆掉／毀壞」。
generatedExample: The old theater is still standing after the earthquake.

explanationZhTw: 表示「在……旁邊；緊鄰著」，是常用的空間介系詞／副詞。
generatedExample: There is a small cafe alongside the library.

explanationZhTw: 指房子前方有遮棚的入口平台或門廊。
generatedExample: We sat on the porch and watched the rain.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 flight 不是「飛行」，而是指「一段／一組連續的階梯」。
generatedExample: The museum has a long flight of steps leading to the entrance.

explanationZhTw: 「propaganda」在這裡是指政治宣傳、洗腦式的資訊傳播，帶有操控輿論的意味。
generatedExample: The poster was part of a propaganda campaign.

explanationZhTw: 「scale models」是按比例縮小製作的模型。
generatedExample: We studied the city using scale models in class.

explanationZhTw: 「waxwork tableaux」指用蠟像排成的場景展示；tableaux 原本是刻意擺設的靜態畫面。
generatedExample: The museum featured waxwork tableaux of famous historical scenes.

explanationZhTw: 「atrocities」是指殘暴行為或暴行，常用來描述戰爭中的嚴重罪行。
generatedExample: Witnesses described the atrocities committed during the war.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般的「補充、增補」名詞用法，而是指他在說完前一句後又接著插話、補上一句。
generatedExample: 'I think it's closed now,' supplemented Maria, 'but I am not sure.'

explanationZhTw: 書面語，意思是「記得、回想起來」。比一般的 remember 更正式。
generatedExample: I can't recollect where I put my keys.

explanationZhTw: 「incongruous」表示「不協調的、格格不入的」。這裡指這幅畫作為私人物品很不合適、很突兀。
generatedExample: A bright neon sign would be an incongruous possession in a small stone cottage.

explanationZhTw: 「linger」是「逗留、流連」。這裡不是單純待著，而是因為還想多看一會兒、多聊幾句，所以沒有立刻離開。
generatedExample: She lingered at the doorway, reluctant to leave the party.

explanationZhTw: 「inhabit」在這裡是比較正式的「居住、棲身於」。用在店鋪上時，帶有文學式或正式語氣。
generatedExample: The family had inhabited the house for three generations.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，意思是「一直在腦中反覆浮現、縈繞心頭」。不是字面上真的在跑。
generatedExample: That line from the song kept running through my head all morning.

explanationZhTw: 「have the illusion of」表示「產生錯覺，以為自己……」。這裡指他讀出來時，好像真的聽見了鐘聲。
generatedExample: In the dark, he had the illusion of seeing someone standing by the window.

explanationZhTw: 「peal forth」是較文學、較少見的說法，指鐘聲「響起、傳出」。
generatedExample: At noon, the church bells pealed forth across the valley.

explanationZhTw: 「got away from」在這裡是「離開、擺脫」的意思，不是字面上的「逃走」那麼強烈；表示他成功離開了 Mr Charrington 身邊。
generatedExample: She got away from the crowd and found a quiet seat.

explanationZhTw: 「reconnoitring」原本是軍事用語，意思是「偵察、先行探查」；這裡表示他先觀察街上的情況，再決定要不要出去。
generatedExample: The hikers spent a few minutes reconnoitring the trail before starting the climb.

explanationZhTw: 「made up his mind」是固定說法，意思是「下定決心、決定好」。
generatedExample: I finally made up my mind to leave the job.

explanationZhTw: 「shirking」是「逃避、偷懶地不去做應做的事」；這裡指故意不去參加在 Centre 的晚間活動。
generatedExample: He was shirking his homework again.

explanationZhTw: 「folly」在這裡不是普通的「愚蠢」形容，而是指「愚蠢的行為、蠢事」；整個片語是在說「最嚴重的蠢事」。
generatedExample: Buying the car without checking it was a piece of folly.

explanationZhTw: 「proprietor」是較正式的字，意思是「店主、業主」。
generatedExample: The proprietor closed the shop early that day.

explanationZhTw: `come back` 在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「回來、再回到原處」；不是字面上只指身體回返，也可帶有重返某事的意思。
generatedExample: I need to come back later to pick up my coat.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `drag ... out of` 不是單純「拖出來」，而是「費力地逼出、設法挖出」；指他想從對方記憶裡套出那首詩的其餘部分。
generatedExample: It took hours to drag the truth out of him.

explanationZhTw: `lunatic` 在這裡不是指醫學上的「精神病患」，而是表示「荒唐的、瘋狂的、不切實際的」；整個片語是在說一個很離譜的計畫。
generatedExample: It was a lunatic idea to drive all night after no sleep.

explanationZhTw: `exaltation` 指強烈的興奮、狂喜或亢奮；這裡表示他因為情緒高漲而變得大意。
generatedExample: Her exaltation after winning the race was obvious.

explanationZhTw: `without so much as` 是慣用說法，意思是「甚至連……都沒有」；語氣比普通的 `without` 更強，表示完全沒做某個最基本的動作。
generatedExample: He left without so much as saying goodbye.

explanationZhTw: `improvised` 在這裡是「即興編出的、臨場想出來的」；`improvised tune` 就是現場隨手哼出的旋律。
generatedExample: He played an improvised tune on the piano.

explanationZhTw: 這是童謠《Oranges and Lemons》中的固定唱詞。這裡的「say」不是單純的「說」，而是童謠裡用來引出鐘聲或唱誦節奏的用法，帶有擬人化和吟唱感。
generatedExample: The chorus ended with the line, "say the bells of St Clement's," which gave the song a folk-rhyme feel.

explanationZhTw: farthings 是英國舊時的貨幣單位「法尋」，金額非常小而且現在已不常用。這裡是指一筆極小的欠款，也帶有年代感。
generatedExample: My grandfather kept an old farthing in a jar as a reminder of how money used to look.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是慣用說法，表示「嚇得發冷、心裡發寒」；不是字面上的真的變成冰。
generatedExample: When she saw the test results, her heart seemed to turn to ice.

explanationZhTw: 這是強烈恐懼的比喻說法，意思是「嚇到腸胃發軟、膽戰心驚」。
generatedExample: The noise from the basement made my bowels turn to water.

explanationZhTw: 英式英語中指「人行道」；在美式英語較常說 `sidewalk`。
generatedExample: We walked along the pavement after dinner.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「直視他的臉、正面地看著他」，強調沒有閃避。
generatedExample: He looked me straight in the face and said he was sorry.

explanationZhTw: 太麻痺、僵住到動不了。這裡表示溫斯頓因為震驚或恐懼而無法行動。
generatedExample: I was too paralysed to move when I heard the strange noise behind me.

explanationZhTw: 走錯方向。這裡是字面意思，但也可引申為做法或判斷出了差錯。
generatedExample: We realized we were going in the wrong direction and turned back.

explanationZhTw: 不管怎樣；無論如何。用來把話題拉回重點，表示前面細節先不管。
generatedExample: At any rate, we need to finish the report today.

explanationZhTw: 監視、暗中跟蹤。`spy on someone` 是片語動詞，表示秘密觀察某人。
generatedExample: Someone was spying on us from across the street.

explanationZhTw: 可信的；合理的。這裡表示「碰巧出現在同一條路上」這件事不太可能是真的。
generatedExample: His explanation sounded credible, so we believed him.

explanationZhTw: 巧合；湊巧發生的事。這裡強調事情同時出現得太剛好，反而令人懷疑。
generatedExample: Meeting her again in another city felt like a strange coincidence.

explanationZhTw: 特務、密探；此處不是一般「代理人」，而是指替某個組織秘密行動的人。
generatedExample: He was accused of being an agent of the enemy.

explanationZhTw: 「effort」指費力、很難做成的動作；這裡是說走路本身都很吃力。
generatedExample: After the illness, even climbing stairs felt like an effort.

explanationZhTw: 「half minded to」表示有點想要、幾乎打算要做某事，但還沒有真的下決定。
generatedExample: I was half minded to stay home, but I went to the meeting anyway.

explanationZhTw: 「throw away」是片語動詞，意思是把東西丟掉、扔掉。
generatedExample: The box was broken, so I threw it away.

explanationZhTw: 「pain」是疼痛、痛楚。
generatedExample: She felt a sharp pain in her shoulder.

explanationZhTw: 「belly」是肚子、腹部，比較口語。
generatedExample: The baby kicked her belly.

explanationZhTw: 「lavatory」是廁所、洗手間，偏英式或較正式的說法。
generatedExample: Excuse me, where is the lavatory?

explanationZhTw: 「spasm」是痙攣、抽搐；這裡指突然一陣不受控制的疼痛或收縮。
generatedExample: He had a spasm in his leg after running too hard.

explanationZhTw: 「dull ache」在這裡不是「遲鈍的疼痛」直譯，而是指持續、鈍鈍的不尖銳痛感。
generatedExample: I had a dull ache in my back all afternoon.

explanationZhTw: 「blind alley」在這裡指「死巷、死路」，不是字面上「看不見的巷子」。常用來描述沒有出口或沒有進展的地方，也可比喻陷入僵局。
generatedExample: We walked into a blind alley and had to turn back.

explanationZhTw: 「halted」在這裡是「突然停下來、停住」的意思，語氣比一般的「stop」更正式，帶有明顯的動作中斷感。
generatedExample: The driver halted when he saw the red light.

explanationZhTw: 「retrace his steps」是慣用語，意思是「沿原路折返；回頭走自己剛才走過的路」。在這裡強調他決定倒回去。
generatedExample: I forgot my phone, so I had to retrace my steps to the office.

explanationZhTw: 「occurred to him」在這裡是「他突然想到」的意思，不是單純「發生」。它表示一個念頭浮現在腦中。
generatedExample: It occurred to me that we had already met before.

explanationZhTw: 「catch up with」是片語動詞，意思是「追上」。這裡指他如果跑起來，可能就能追上那個女孩。
generatedExample: If I leave now, I can catch up with you at the station.

explanationZhTw: 「abandoned the idea」在這裡是「放棄了這個念頭」；不是丟掉實體物件，而是立刻不再打算這麼做。
generatedExample: She abandoned the idea after realizing it would take too long.

explanationZhTw: 「unbearable」意思是「難以忍受的、受不了的」。這裡強調連想到要用力都讓他無法承受。
generatedExample: The heat was unbearable in the crowded room.

explanationZhTw: 「strike a blow」是慣用語，字面是「揮出一擊」，這裡引申為「出手打人、攻擊」。
generatedExample: He wanted to strike a blow, but he was too exhausted.

explanationZhTw: 「lusty」在這裡不是「好色」的意思，而是較文學、較古老的用法，指「強健有活力的、精力旺盛的」。
generatedExample: The old story described him as a lusty young sailor.

explanationZhTw: 「partial alibi」在這裡是「不完全但勉強可用的不在場證明」。他打算用待在社區中心來製造一個部分掩護。
generatedExample: He arrived late, so his alibi was only partial.

explanationZhTw: 「lassitude」是「倦怠、疲乏無力」的較正式用詞，語氣比普通的 tired 更強，也更文學化。
generatedExample: After the long journey, he felt a deep lassitude.

explanationZhTw: 「taken hold of」是片語動詞，意思是「完全控制住、強烈攫住」。這裡表示倦怠感已經把他整個人抓住了。
generatedExample: Fear took hold of the crowd as the siren got louder.

explanationZhTw: got back 表示「回來、回到原處」。
generatedExample: I got back home very late.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 flat 是英式用法，指「公寓／住處」，不是「平坦的」。
generatedExample: She went back to her flat after work.

explanationZhTw: alcove 指牆邊凹進去的小空間，可譯作「壁龕、凹室、小角落」。
generatedExample: They put a small desk in the alcove by the window.

explanationZhTw: at once 表示「立刻、馬上」。
generatedExample: Please reply at once if the plan changes.

explanationZhTw: 這是小說中的裝置名詞，指兼具播放與監控功能的螢幕。
generatedExample: The telescreen crackled to life before breakfast.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 brassy 不是「黃銅的」，而是形容聲音粗硬、響亮、刺耳。
generatedExample: Her brassy voice carried across the room.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `come for you` 不是「來找你」，而是指「來抓你、逮捕你、帶走你」，有明顯威脅意味。
generatedExample: The police came for him before dawn.

explanationZhTw: `disappearances` 在這裡不是單純「失蹤」，而是指被祕密帶走、消失在公眾視線中的事件，帶有政治壓迫語境。
generatedExample: The disappearances made people afraid to speak openly.

explanationZhTw: `unprocurable` 表示「無法取得、買不到、弄不到」，是較正式且少見的字。
generatedExample: During the shortage, medicine became unprocurable.

explanationZhTw: `freezes into inertia` 這裡不是字面上的「結冰」，而是指人在關鍵時刻突然失去行動力、動彈不得。
generatedExample: When the alarm sounded, I froze into inertia for a second.

explanationZhTw: `a sour stomach` 不是「酸的胃」，而是口語中指胃不舒服、消化不良或噁心。
generatedExample: I had a sour stomach after eating too much spicy food.

explanationZhTw: 日記；指用來記錄日常想法或事件的筆記本。
generatedExample: I write in my diary every night.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》裡的監視螢幕；既能播放內容，也帶有監控功能。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the room never turned off.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「插進去」，而是指聲音強烈地卡在腦中，怎麼都忘不掉。
generatedExample: That melody stuck into my brain for hours.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的「思想警察」，指監視、懲罰人們思想的機構。
generatedExample: Everyone feared the Thought Police.

explanationZhTw: 把他帶走，通常暗示是被逮捕、押走或強行帶離。
generatedExample: The officers took him away after the protest.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「立刻、馬上」。
generatedExample: Come at once if you need help.

explanationZhTw: 必須經歷或走完某個程序；這裡指被迫接受整套供認過程。
generatedExample: I have gone through this report twice.

explanationZhTw: 卑躬屈膝、低聲下氣地求饒；帶有很強的屈辱感。
generatedExample: He spent the afternoon grovelling after his mistake.

explanationZhTw: 寬恕、饒命；在這裡是求對方不要繼續施加傷害。
generatedExample: The prisoner begged for mercy.

explanationZhTw: 「endure」指「忍受、熬過」，這裡不是一般的「持續」，而是帶有被迫承受痛苦、壓力或折磨的意思。
generatedExample: She had to endure long hours of uncertainty before the results arrived.

explanationZhTw: 「cut ... out of your life」在這裡是比喻用法，意思是把某段時間從人生中刪掉或省略掉。它不是字面上的「剪掉」，而是說讓那段時間不必經歷。
generatedExample: I wish I could cut those wasted months out of my life.

explanationZhTw: 「escape detection」是固定搭配，意思是「不被發現、逃過查覺」。這裡強調在監控下不可能躲過搜查或識別。
generatedExample: The faulty device escaped detection for weeks.

explanationZhTw: 「succumb」原意是「屈服、敗給」，這裡表示一旦「屈服於」思想罪，就註定走向毀滅。屬於較正式、偏書面語的用法。
generatedExample: He succumbed to pressure and agreed to the plan.

explanationZhTw: 「embedded in future time」是文學性、非字面用法，表示那種恐懼像被固定在未來之中，無法擺脫。這裡的「embedded」不是技術上的「嵌入」，而是強調深深鑲進時間裡。
generatedExample: The memory seemed embedded in future time, waiting to surface again.

explanationZhTw: 「summon up」在這裡是「努力喚起、回想起」的意思，常用來表示刻意把記憶或畫面從腦中找出來。
generatedExample: I tried to summon up the courage to speak.

explanationZhTw: 「foreknowledge」指「預先知道、先見之明」，這裡帶有帶預言色彩的意味，不只是一般的「知道」，而是對未來的某種先行認知。
generatedExample: With foreknowledge of the storm, they moved the boat to safety.

explanationZhTw: 「telescreen」是小說中的特殊名詞，指一種兼具螢幕與監控功能的裝置；在這裡屬於作品內的設定詞，並非日常通用詞。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the room never stopped watching everyone.

explanationZhTw: 「train of thought」是慣用語，表示「思路、一連串的思考」。這裡指他被打斷後，無法繼續剛才的想法。
generatedExample: Sorry, you interrupted my train of thought.

explanationZhTw: 「swam into his mind」是帶有畫面感的說法，表示某個影像或念頭「突然浮現在腦海中」。不是字面上的游進去。
generatedExample: A strange memory swam into her mind as she walked home.

explanationZhTw: 「leaden knell」是比喻說法，指沉重、陰鬱、像喪鐘一樣令人不安的聲音或訊息。這裡強調話語帶來的壓迫感。
generatedExample: The announcement arrived like a leaden knell for the whole team.

explanationZhTw: 這句是反諷式口號。意思不是字面上的「戰爭就是和平」，而是政權用戰爭來維持秩序、控制人民，讓人誤以為外在敵對可以帶來內部穩定。
generatedExample: The slogan WAR IS PEACE shows how the regime twists language to control people.

explanationZhTw: 這句是反諷式口號。它表達的是：政權把真正的自由說成危險的東西，暗示若沒有服從，人民反而會陷入痛苦或受支配的狀態。
generatedExample: The phrase FREEDOM IS SLAVERY makes the message sound like freedom is a threat.

explanationZhTw: 這句是反諷式口號。字面上是「無知就是力量」，但真正意思是政權希望人民不要思考、不要知道真相，因為這樣更容易被控制。
generatedExample: IGNORANCE IS STRENGTH is used to justify keeping people uninformed.
